title: Implement OpenAI Realtime gpt-realtime-2 cloud-native S2S provider
description:
ADR-0002 records the decision to add OpenAI Realtime gpt-realtime-2 as a second cloud-native speech-to-speech provider alongside Gemini Live. No backend code exists today — only the ADR.

Scope:
- New module src-tauri/src/openai_realtime/ mirroring gemini/mod.rs shape (WebSocket session, session resumption, reconnect with backoff, error classification, usage tracking).
- Settings enum entry under RealtimeAgent so the UI can route to it without touching ASR/LLM provider enums.
- Tauri commands: start_openai_realtime / stop_openai_realtime, parallel to start_gemini / stop_gemini.
- Frontend event subscription for the new provider events.
- Tests at the same level as gemini::tests (session_task_cancels_during_reconnect_backoff, client_new_is_disconnected, etc.).

References:
- docs/adr/0002-openai-realtime-provider.md
- docs/adr/0003-speech-to-speech-agent-provider-matrix.md
- src-tauri/src/gemini/mod.rs (~90KB; pattern to follow)
- https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-realtime-2

Out of scope: barge-in, eager turn detection (separate issues).

title: Add TtsProvider trait + Deepgram Aura streaming TTS
description:
ADR-0003 names TTS as a first-class provider category for the speech-to-speech personality — Deepgram Aura, Kokoro, Piper, Coqui — but no implementation exists. TTS is the missing leg of the local/hybrid S2S pipeline (STT -> vLLM -> TTS).

Scope:
- New module src-tauri/src/tts/ with a TtsProvider trait. Stream-shaped (chunked PCM audio with playback timing, support cancellation/barge-in).
- First implementation: DeepgramAura over WebSocket, parallel to existing asr/deepgram.rs.
- Settings enum entry TtsProvider with variants None / DeepgramAura / Local* (stubbed).
- Wire provider into the S2S turn orchestrator (separate issue) once that lands.
- Tauri commands: test_tts_connection_cmd, list_tts_voices_cmd.
- Frontend types + credentials allowlist sync.

References:
- docs/adr/0003-speech-to-speech-agent-provider-matrix.md (provider matrix)
- src-tauri/src/asr/deepgram.rs (54KB; shape to mirror)
- https://developers.deepgram.com/docs/tts-websocket

Out of scope: Kokoro/Piper/Coqui local TTS engines (separate issues), barge-in semantics (separate issue).

title: Local/hybrid S2S pipeline: STT -> vLLM -> TTS turn orchestrator
description:
ADR-0003 wave 3 — composed-pipeline speech-to-speech for users who want local/hybrid voice agents.

Scope:
- New module src-tauri/src/s2s_local/ with a TurnState struct (source id, provider ids, sample rate, start timestamp, cancellation token, bounded buffers).
- Turn protocol: turn_start, audio append, turn_end, cancel. (barge_in is a separate issue.)
- Wires existing ASR finals -> existing OpenAI-compatible LLM (vLLM via HTTP) -> new TtsProvider.
- Latency milestone events: turn start, first STT partial, STT final, LLM request start, first LLM token, first TTS audio, final TTS audio, cancel ack.
- Frontend: surface latency milestones beside existing pipeline-latency status.
- Tauri commands: start_local_s2s, stop_local_s2s.

Hard dependency: the TtsProvider trait + at least one implementation must land first.

References:
- docs/adr/0003 turn protocol section
- ../HF/streaming-speech-to-speech (HF reference impl; do not port wholesale)

title: Local TTS providers: Kokoro, Piper, Coqui
description:
Once TtsProvider trait lands, add local TTS engines for fully local S2S pipeline. Each is a separate impl behind a cargo feature flag (tts-kokoro, tts-piper, tts-coqui).

Scope (per provider):
- ONNX or other binding crate selection.
- Voice model bundling / download script update (scripts/download-models.ps1).
- Streaming chunk emission compatible with TtsProvider trait.

Out of scope: TtsProvider trait itself (separate issue).

title: Deepgram Flux EagerEndOfTurn support with TurnResumed rollback
description:
ADR-0003 'Deepgram + local turn strategy' notes EagerEndOfTurn can shave latency by starting LLM/TTS work before the high-confidence EndOfTurn, with TurnResumed as the rollback signal.

Scope:
- Wire Flux events through to S2S turn orchestrator.
- On EagerEndOfTurn: start LLM/TTS speculatively; capture cancel token.
- On TurnResumed: cancel speculative LLM/TTS work, wait for next final/eager event.
- UI surfaces eager-turn count + false-start (TurnResumed) count so users can tune.
- Telemetry: emit per-turn 'speculative cancelled' events for latency dashboards.

Hard dependency: S2S turn orchestrator must exist first.

title: Barge-in / interruption support across S2S providers
description:
ADR-0003 wave 5 — interruption semantics for Gemini Live, OpenAI Realtime, and the local pipeline.

Scope:
- Cancel current LLM/TTS output when a new turn starts.
- Stop UI playback immediately.
- Emit cancel-acknowledgement event before allowing the next turn.
- Immediate-retry guards so user can't double-trigger.
- Per-provider implementation: each S2S provider needs its own cancel path.

Hard dependency: at least the turn protocol from the local S2S epic.

title: Add cargo tauri build smoke job to CI
description:
Today CI never validates the production-bundle path. cargo check / cargo test exercise the lib crate, but bundling can quietly regress until release time.

Scope:
- New CI job 'release-bundle' (or extend the existing release.yml) that runs 'bun run tauri build' on each platform.
- Gate to push-on-master only (don't run on every PR — too slow/expensive).
- Cache target/ aggressively across runs.
- Fail-fast: a bundle failure should land in the same actionable place as cargo failures.

References:
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (current jobs)
- .github/workflows/release.yml (existing release pipeline; may already have most of this)

title: Flip clippy to -D warnings once tree is clean
description:
Wave 1 added a lints job that runs cargo clippy --all-targets in warnings-only mode. Once we confirm the tree is clippy-clean (or fix the existing warnings), flip to '-- -D warnings' so clippy regressions can't merge.

Scope:
- Inspect current clippy output on Linux job once it's green.
- Fix any latent warnings.
- Update .github/workflows/ci.yml lints job to add '-D warnings' to the clippy invocation.

This is a trivial follow-up to commit 7fd608f.

title: Replace pinned pipewire-debian PPA with stock Ubuntu 24.04 packages or pin to PPA SHA
description:
ci.yml uses 'sudo add-apt-repository ppa:pipewire-debian/pipewire-upstream -y' on every Linux job (rust-linux + lints). This is an unpinned third-party PPA — whoever controls that account can replace pipewire packages on every CI run.

Scope:
- Test whether stock Ubuntu 24.04 'libpipewire-0.3-dev' satisfies rsac's minimum version. If yes, drop the PPA entirely.
- If not, pin a specific version with 'apt-get install libpipewire-0.3-dev=<exact>' or vendor the .deb files.

Found by the Linux CI auditor agent during Wave 1.

title: Migrate CI to Node.js 24 actions before September 2026
description:
GitHub deprecation warning on every CI run today: 'Node.js 20 actions are deprecated. Will be removed September 16, 2026.'

Affected actions in our workflow:
- actions/checkout
- ilammy/msvc-dev-cmd

Scope:
- Watch upstream for Node.js 24 releases of these actions.
- Bump SHA pins when available.
- Or set FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24=true env to opt in early.

Date: hard deadline 2026-09-16.

title: Track actual token usage for chat/LLM responses
description:
src-tauri/src/commands.rs has a '// TODO: track actual token usage' comment. send_chat_message returns hard-coded tokens_used: 0.

Scope:
- Plumb token counts from each LLM provider (LocalLlama, Api/OpenAI-compat, AwsBedrock, MistralRs) through to ChatMessage.tokens_used.
- Match the pattern Gemini Live already uses (it tracks usage via events).
- Ensure get_session_usage / get_lifetime_usage / seed_lifetime_migration aggregations include LLM tokens.

Found by project-mapping agent during Wave 1 audit.

title: Tighten unwrap/expect calls in commands.rs
description:
Project-mapping audit identified 22 unwrap/expect calls in src-tauri/src/commands.rs — mostly in thread-pool init and parsing. Not panic-prone in practice but could be more defensive.

Scope:
- Audit each unwrap/expect.
- Convert to '?' / Result-returning code where the call site can plausibly fail at runtime.
- Leave unwraps that are genuinely infallible (e.g. inside lazy_static, after compile-time-checked constants) with a comment explaining why.

title: Document GraphDelta dual-emission contract or unify to single channel
description:
Project-mapping audit found backend emits both full graph-update snapshots (~10s throttle) AND incremental graph-delta events (per-extraction). UI may be optimized for deltas; dual-emission could cause reconciliation bugs.

Scope:
- Decide: keep both (with explicit contract) or consolidate to delta-only with on-demand snapshot.
- If keeping both: write src-tauri/src/events.rs doc-comment specifying the ordering invariant (e.g. 'a snapshot supersedes any deltas with timestamp <= snapshot.timestamp').
- Frontend: verify the React graph store applies them in the right order; add a vitest if not covered.

Found during Wave 1 mapping; surfaced as an architectural risk, not a confirmed bug.

title: Switch rsac path-dep to crates.io once rsac v0.2.0 ships
description:
Cargo.toml comments at lines 38-53 already plan this: 'After rsac 0.2.0 publishes to crates.io, flip each target block to the crates.io dep.' Today we use 'rsac = { path = "../../rsac" }' per-platform.

Scope:
- Wait for / coordinate rsac v0.2.0 release.
- Flip each target block in src-tauri/Cargo.toml.
- Drop the sibling-clone steps in .github/workflows/ci.yml (RSAC_REPO_URL / RSAC_REPO_SHA).
- Update local-dev docs in README to remove the sibling-checkout requirement.

Cross-repo coordination: requires action in the rsac repo first.

title: Move set_var(GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS) out of Vertex AI hot path
description:
Test suite auditor flagged src-tauri/src/gemini/mod.rs:762 — production code calls 'set_var(GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS, ...)' inside the VertexAI auth path. This mutates process-global env without restoring; any test that exercises Vertex auth leaks the value.

Scope:
- Replace with per-request credential file path passed via Vertex SDK config object, not env var.
- Or, if the Google auth library only reads the env var, isolate the mutation behind a Mutex + restore-on-drop guard.

Severity: medium — currently no test hits this path, but it's a latent landmine.
